name: Git Config Fields

tasks.title: Add new git config fields to GitConfig struct
tasks.description: Add new git config fields to GitConfig struct
tasks.body:
Add three new boolean fields to the GitConfig struct in internal/config/types.go:

- CommitOnSuccess (json: commit_on_success) - controls whether daemon commits after successful task completion
- CommitOnFailure (json: commit_on_failure) - controls whether daemon commits partial work after task failure
- CommitState (json: commit_state) - controls whether .wolfcastle/ state is included in daemon commits

These join the existing AutoCommit (master switch) and SkipHooksOnAutoCommit fields. When AutoCommit is false, the fine-grained controls are ignored.

Also update Defaults() in internal/config/config.go to set all three new fields to true by default.

Note: the spec calls for a skip_hooks field, but SkipHooksOnAutoCommit already serves this purpose. Do not rename or duplicate it.
acceptance_criteria: GitConfig struct has all three new fields with correct json tags
Defaults() returns true for all three new fields

tasks.title: Add validation for new git config fields
tasks.description: Add validation for new git config fields
tasks.body:
Update internal/config/validate.go to validate the new git config fields. The validation logic should ensure:

- No validation errors when auto_commit is false (fine-grained fields are ignored)
- When auto_commit is true, commit_on_success and commit_on_failure are booleans (no special validation needed beyond type checking from JSON unmarshal)
- The existing validation for commit_message_format remains unchanged

Add or update unit tests in the config validation test file to cover the new fields, including edge cases where auto_commit is false and the sub-fields have various values.
acceptance_criteria: Validation passes for valid configs with new fields

tasks.title: Update config-reference.md with new git fields
tasks.description: Update config-reference.md with new git fields
tasks.body:
Update docs/humans/config-reference.md to document the three new git config fields in the git section (currently lines 388-428):

- git.commit_on_success: boolean, default true. Commit after successful task completion. Only applies when auto_commit is true.
- git.commit_on_failure: boolean, default true. Commit partial work after task failure. Only applies when auto_commit is true.
- git.commit_state: boolean, default true. Include .wolfcastle/ state directory in daemon commits. When false, only code changes are committed. Only applies when auto_commit is true.

Follow the existing format in the config reference for consistency.
acceptance_criteria: All three new fields documented with types
Follows existing format in the git section

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Git Config Fields is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify GitConfig struct has CommitOnSuccess, CommitOnFailure, CommitState fields with correct json tags
Verify Defaults() sets all three new fields to true

breadcrumbs.text: Added CommitOnSuccess, CommitOnFailure, CommitState to GitConfig struct and Defaults(). Build and tests pass.
Added table-driven validation tests for CommitOnSuccess, CommitOnFailure, CommitState field combinations. No validation logic changes needed; booleans are self-validating via JSON unmarshal.
Documented commit_on_success, commit_on_failure, commit_state in docs/humans/config-reference.md following existing git section format.

Audit reviewed 4 files: types.go (3 new bool fields on GitConfig), config.go (defaults set to true), validate_extra_test.go (table-driven test covering 8 field combinations plus format-unchanged regression), config-reference.md (3 new entries following established format). PASS. Zero findings. Build clean, all tests pass including race detector, gofmt and go vet clean. Booleans require no validation beyond JSON unmarshal, which is correctly handled. Defaults, struct placement, test coverage, and documentation are all consistent and correct. Quality: clean, minimal, well-scoped work across all three tasks.

audit.result_summary: Added three granular commit-control boolean fields (CommitOnSuccess, CommitOnFailure, CommitState) to GitConfig, all defaulting to true and gated by the existing AutoCommit master switch. The struct, defaults, validation tests (8-combination table-driven suite), and config-reference documentation are complete and consistent. No validation logic was needed beyond JSON unmarshal for boolean types. All tests pass, including race detector. The node is ready for downstream consumption by the daemon-commit-logic node.

audit.objective: Verify all work in the git-config-fields node is complete and correct
audit.what_happened: Reviewed types.go, config.go, validate_extra_test.go, validate.go, and config-reference.md. Ran full build, all tests, race detector, gofmt, and go vet. All pass with zero issues. Confirmed struct fields are correctly placed, defaults match documentation, tests cover the relevant matrix, and no validation logic is needed for plain booleans.
went_well: The three tasks were well-scoped and executed cleanly; each change was minimal and correct
Table-driven test design in validate_extra_test.go covers combinations without bloating the test file
improvements: None identified; the scope was small enough that there is little room for error
action_items: Downstream daemon-commit-logic node should honor auto_commit as master switch for the three sub-fields at runtime

task-0001.objective: Add three new git config fields (CommitOnSuccess, CommitOnFailure, CommitState) to GitConfig struct and wire defaults
task-0001.what_happened: Added three bool fields to GitConfig in types.go with correct json tags. Set all three to true in Defaults() in config.go. Placement groups the new fields with the existing AutoCommit master switch for readability.
went_well: Minimal change, clean build, all existing tests pass without modification
improvements: None identified; this was a small, scoped struct change
action_items: Downstream tasks will need to read these fields in the daemon commit logic and add validation if AutoCommit=false should suppress them

task-0002.objective: Add validation and tests for the new git config boolean fields
task-0002.what_happened: The three new fields (CommitOnSuccess, CommitOnFailure, CommitState) are all booleans, so JSON unmarshal handles type enforcement. No changes to validate.go were needed. Added a table-driven test covering 8 combinations of auto_commit with the sub-fields, plus a test confirming commit_message_format validation is unaffected by the new fields.
went_well: Table-driven test covers the matrix cleanly in a single test function
All existing tests continue to pass unchanged
improvements: If future fields under git config require range or enum validation, the table-driven test structure makes it easy to extend
action_items: Downstream daemon commit logic should honor the auto_commit master switch to suppress sub-fields at runtime, not at validation time

task-0003.objective: Document the three new git config fields in config-reference.md
task-0003.what_happened: Added documentation for commit_on_success, commit_on_failure, and commit_state to the git section of docs/humans/config-reference.md. Placed them after skip_hooks_on_auto_commit, following the established format (Type, Default, Description bullet points). Each entry notes the auto_commit dependency.
went_well: Existing format was consistent and easy to match
action_items: None; all three git config fields are now fully implemented, tested, and documented
